pr.title: lb: Partially match lbarq and lbmthp functions
pr.author: itsgrimetime

summary: Partially decompiled two library-area functions: lbArq_80014AC4 in the ARQ helper code and fn_8001EBF0 in THP/MTHP code. The PR replaced placeholder prototypes with typed structs and signatures, added ARQ list-management logic guarded by OS interrupt disable/restore calls, and introduced a THPDecComp layout sufficient to express initialization/size-calculation behavior in fn_8001EBF0.
what_changed: Implemented lbArq_80014AC4(lbArqHandle* handle), replacing the placeholder marker.
Added ARQ-related types in lbarq.h: lbArqCallback, lbArqNode, lbArqGlobal, and lbArqHandle.
Declared extern lbArqGlobal lbArq_804316C0 and included <dolphin/ar.h> so ARQRequest can be represented in the node layout.
lbArq_80014AC4 now removes a node from its current state-indexed list, appends it to done list index 2, restores interrupts, optionally invokes a callback, then moves the node to free list index 0.
Included <dolphin/os.h> in lbarq.c to use OSDisableInterrupts and OSRestoreInterrupts around list mutation.
Implemented fn_8001EBF0(THPDecComp* data), replacing the placeholder marker.
Added ALIGN_32 macro locally in lbmthp.c for 0x20-byte alignment calculations.
Added a partial THPDecComp struct in lbmthp.h with explicit padding and known fields from offsets 0x40 through 0x104.
Declared THPDec_8032FD40(void* arg0, u16 height), used by fn_8001EBF0 in its size calculation.
Updated fn_8001EBF0 prototype from UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS to s32 fn_8001EBF0(THPDecComp* data).
smart_moves: Used a minimal offset-accurate struct for THPDecComp rather than over-naming fields without evidence; most unknown fields remain unk_* with offset-based names.
Preserved apparent instruction-order sensitivity in fn_8001EBF0 by explicitly loading width, unk_100, height, and unk_104 before stores and arithmetic, as shown by the comments in the diff.
Modeled lbArq_80014AC4 list manipulation with pointer-to-pointer traversal, which cleanly represents removal from a singly linked list without special-casing the head node.
Kept interrupt masking localized to list mutations while restoring interrupts before invoking the callback, avoiding holding the critical section during arbitrary callback code.
Represented ARQ node state through list indices and state values, including moving to done list 2 and free list 0, which captures concrete behavior without inventing semantic enum names.
decomp_lessons: For partial matches, replacing UNK_PARAMS with a narrowly justified struct pointer can unlock meaningful C while still leaving unknown fields named by offset.
When a function mutates global intrusive linked lists, pointer-to-pointer local variables can match both behavior and likely compiler codegen better than branch-heavy head/tail special cases.
Callback boundaries matter: list state may need to be updated before the callback, then reacquired under interrupts afterward for final cleanup.
Alignment helpers such as (((x) + 0x1F) & ~0x1F) are useful for matching media/decoder buffer sizing code that rounds to 32-byte boundaries.
Explicit padding arrays in headers are acceptable when the PR has evidence only for selected offsets; this keeps later decomp work extensible without forcing unsupported field names.
Function-local comments documenting load/store order can be useful in matching-sensitive code, especially when the C expression order affects generated assembly.
naming_conventions: Library functions retain address-based names such as lbArq_80014AC4 and fn_8001EBF0 when semantic names are not yet established.
Unknown struct fields are named with offset-bearing identifiers such as unk_40, unk_A0, unk_100, and unk_104.
Padding fields are named by offset ranges, such as pad4C, pad6C, pad8C, padA5, and padAD.
Callback typedef uses subsystem prefix and role: lbArqCallback.
Reconstructed structs use subsystem/type names such as lbArqNode, lbArqGlobal, lbArqHandle, and THPDecComp.
Global symbol keeps the decomp symbol/address form lbArq_804316C0.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Used a 32-byte alignment macro, ALIGN_32(x), equivalent to add 0x1F then mask with ~0x1F.
Kept fn_8001EBF0 operations in a commented sequence that mirrors likely load, align, store, multiply, reload, and accumulate order.
Used u16 casts for width and height stores/calls where the target fields or callee argument width appear to require halfword behavior.
Accumulated size step-by-step, including width*height, two copies of wh >> 2, THPDec_8032FD40 return value, and aligned table sizes from unk_104 * 4 and unk_40 * 4.
Used pointer-to-pointer traversal for singly linked list removal and tail append in lbArq_80014AC4.
Guarded ARQ global list updates with OSDisableInterrupts and OSRestoreInterrupts to match platform synchronization behavior.
review_feedback: No review comments were present in the provided PR slice.
No human PR body text was included in the provided excerpt, so rationale beyond the diff and title is not evidenced.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that rename lbArq_80014AC4 or replace lbArqNode state list indices with an enum.
Find later PRs that expand THPDecComp field names beyond unk_* offsets.
Search for uses of THPDec_8032FD40 to confirm its signature and semantic purpose.
Search for subsequent matching work in src/melee/lb/lbmthp.c around fn_8001EB14, fn_8001ECF4, fn_8001EF5C, and fn_8001F06C.
Search for lbArq_804316C0 definitions to validate lbArqGlobal padding and list offset.
